column1: # RetroMod — Forge SRG → Mojang member-name mappings
# KIND is FIELD or METHOD. Comments start with '#'. Blank lines are ignored.
# Owner class is intentionally NOT recorded — SRG names are unique across the
# entire MC namespace, so a single SRG → Mojang mapping is sufficient regardless
# of the field/method's owner. The transformer's ClassRemapper applies these
# blindly to any matching SRG-pattern reference in the input bytecode.
# Forge mods built with ForgeGradle's reobfJar task (which is essentially every
# Forge mod for MC < 1.20.5) reference MC members by Forge's SRG names — fields
# like Blocks.f_50069_ (= STONE) and methods like Component.m_237113_ (= literal).
# Forge's runtime classloader used to remap these to the actual MC names at
# class load time. Forge 64.x for MC 26.1 dropped the SRG remap layer because
# MC 26.1 has no obfuscation at all (Mojang shipped official names directly).
# Reobf'd mods then break with NoSuchFieldError / NoSuchMethodError on every
# SRG name. RetroMod takes over that responsibility via this dictionary.
# This is a STARTER SET — the highest-value symbols that the test mod and
# common Forge mods (Jade, JEI, JourneyMap) rely on. The full SRG namespace
# spans tens of thousands of entries.
#   1. Download Forge's official SRG → MCP mapping CSV from MinecraftForge maven
#   2. Cross-reference against MC's official Mojang mappings (proguard-style)
# A MappingComposer-style builder for this file is a future enhancement —
# A given SRG name maps to exactly one Mojang name. If two rows declare the
# same SRG key, HashMap.put silently keeps the LAST entry — so duplicates are
# data-loss bugs, not merges. Don't add a row whose KIND+SRG_NAME already
# These are the registry-singleton instances of vanilla Blocks. Reobf'd mods
# reference them as Blocks.f_NNNNN_ instead of Blocks.STONE, etc.
# These are the most common bare-string text constructors. Component is an
# interface (since the 1.19 component refactor), so the methods are static
# factories — very high frequency in mod code (every translation key, every
# literal string sent to chat or shown in a tooltip).
# These are the static parsers/constructors used everywhere mods need to
# reference an MC registry entry by ID. The constructor became private in
# the Identifier refactor, so factories are the only path forward.
# METHODS — Common Entity / Player / LivingEntity getters
